title: taraxacum officinale subsp. paludosum
intro: Taraxacum officinale subsp. paludosum, also known as the marsh dandelion, is a subspecies of the dandelion plant, characterized by its ability to thrive in wet environments. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain compounds like taraxasterol and inulin that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of marsh dandelion include its ability to support liver function, reduce blood pressure, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a useful treatment for conditions like edema and urinary tract infections. Marsh dandelion can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate digestive issues.
